title: Stable view-instance IDs + derived `view` capability kind

tests.summary: 15 passed — ref codec round-trip (default omitted), traversal rejection at codec + path-builder, descriptor/provision derivation (embedded + own-port + none), resolver `view` cardinality:any discovers all derived views, named consumption targets one, backward compat, prompt-block no-pollution, instance enumeration from sandbox GARRISON_HOME. Full suite 477 passed / 3 skipped.

build.summary: isolated dist dir (new next.config distDir env hook) — live dev server's dynamic routes verified 200 after build

e2e.summary: N/A — lib-only slice; behavioral proof is the committed vitest gate

video.note: W-wave consolidated walkthrough (11 beats, all vision-verified, unflagged). Per-slice beats: W1 instance refs visible in pane headers (beat 9); W2 toggles/no-save/restored session (beats 1-5); W3 eager toggles + server-boot terminal (beats 1-4); W4 workspace switch/add-pane/reload/live-terminal-pane (beats 6-9).

notes: Retry 1: first build failed — view-instances.ts originally held node:fs/path helpers and capabilities.ts is client-imported (FittingOverview), pulling node builtins into the client bundle. Fixed by splitting: view-instances.ts stays pure (codec + derivation), disk side (viewStateDir/viewStateFile/listInstanceIds) moved to server-only view-state.ts. Derivation lives in the RESOLVER (synthetic-vault precedent), not metadata.provides, so the assembled prompt's capabilities block is untouched (asserted by test). Side fix: next.config.mjs distDir honours NEXT_DIST_DIR so gate builds never poison the running dev server (friction-log 2026-06-10 permanently resolved).
